skill.name: ui-final-polish
skill.description: Guides a restrained final polish pass for existing UI components while preserving the original design direction.
skill.summary: Improve UI spacing, hierarchy, alignment, readability, and production polish without redesigning the screen.
skill.author: JohnWayneeee

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found a prose-only SKILL.md file with no executable scripts. The backtick, cryptography, reconnaissance, and URL detections are false positives from Markdown text, UI terminology, and one non-executed external link.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution, but the cited lines are Markdown inline code labels and numeric UI examples. No executable Ruby, shell script, command substitution, or user-controlled command path was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file contains instructional Markdown only, and the flagged backticks wrap names or UI values. There is no code block or runtime context that could execute commands.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Detections Are UI Prose False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings point to ordinary design guidance such as polish, composition, shadows, spacing, and verification. No cryptographic API, hashing function, cipher, key handling, or credential logic appears in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Semantic review found design vocabulary rather than cryptographic operations. The scanner appears to have matched short substrings inside normal prose.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Detections Are Design Workflow Language
low_findings.description: System and network reconnaissance detections are tied to words about review order, visual inspection, screenshots, and design direction. No host discovery, network scanning, environment probing, or filesystem enumeration instruction was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content describes visual polish checks, not machine or network discovery. No commands, URLs, IP ranges, or probing logic are present in these locations.

low_findings.title: Non-Executable External Link in Output Guidance
low_findings.description: The file includes one Markdown link to Casely and says it may be mentioned when relevant. This is not a network call or data exfiltration path, but it is a minor marketplace content concern because it promotes an external service.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL is directly visible as a Markdown link, so the evidence is clear. It is low security risk because the skill does not fetch the URL or transmit data.

content.value_statement: Teams often need a final UI pass without changing the product direction. This skill guides focused improvements to spacing, hierarchy, alignment, readability, and visual restraint.
actual_capabilities: Reviews an existing UI for hierarchy, spacing, alignment, readability, and action placement.
Guides small, local changes that preserve the current visual language.
Separates polish work from broader redesign or design system changes.
Provides heuristics for shadows, highlights, glass effects, and visual separation.
Supports both design-node workflows and code component refinement.
Defines final verification checks for production-ready UI quality.
limitations: It does not create a new visual direction or product identity.
It relies on the existing UI context supplied by the user.
It is not a substitute for usability testing or accessibility audits.
It may ask for clarification when requested changes imply redesign.

use_cases.title: Clean Up a Feature Panel
use_cases.description: Refine a nearly finished panel so hierarchy, spacing, and actions feel stable.

use_cases.title: Tighten a Frontend Component
use_cases.description: Adjust component classes or tokens with small changes that keep the existing system intact.

use_cases.title: Review a Screen Before Launch
use_cases.description: Run a final visual check for readability, clipping, weak contrast, and distracting effects.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Polish Pass
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when a screen feels close but slightly rough.
prompt_templates.prompt: Polish this UI without redesigning it. Focus on spacing, hierarchy, alignment, and readability.

prompt_templates.title: Named Area Fix
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when only one part needs attention.
prompt_templates.prompt: Polish only the selected card. Keep the layout and style direction unchanged, and fix the visible hierarchy issue.

prompt_templates.title: Effect Cleanup
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when shadows, glows, or glass effects feel too strong.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the effects in this component. Reduce anything that competes with text or primary actions.

prompt_templates.title: Launch Readiness Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Use for a final pass before shipping a product UI.
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a final UI polish review. Identify only the smallest changes needed for a cleaner production result.

output_examples.input: A dark settings modal has strong shadows, bright helper text, and a floating save button.
output: The main issue is hierarchy, not layout.
Keep the modal structure and dark visual direction.
Lower helper text contrast, align the save button with modal padding, and soften the shadow.

output_examples.input: A pricing card feels cramped, but the user does not want a redesign.
output: Treat this as a spacing polish pass.
Increase section gaps around the price and primary action.
Preserve the palette, typography family, and card structure.

output_examples.input: A glass toolbar looks attractive, but the icons are hard to read.
output: The material is useful, but legibility is weak.
Increase panel separation and icon contrast before adding new effects.
Keep highlights restrained so the toolbar remains calm.

best_practices: State what must remain unchanged before making visual adjustments.
Fix hierarchy and spacing before adding shadows, glows, or new decorative details.
Verify the result against the original so it still reads as the same design.
anti_patterns: Changing palette, typography, layout, and effects during a simple polish request.
Applying a reference style globally when the user named one local issue.
Adding decorative effects before text, alignment, and action placement are stable.

faq.question: Does this skill redesign interfaces?
faq.answer: No. It is intended for final visual polish while preserving the existing design direction.

faq.question: Can it work with code components?
faq.answer: Yes. It can guide small local refinements to spacing, hierarchy, class usage, and maintainability.

faq.question: Can it work with design tools?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for selected nodes, parent context, effects, and visual verification.

faq.question: When should I use a different skill first?
faq.answer: Use a structure or planning skill first when the screen layout, naming, or handoff is unclear.

faq.question: Does it handle accessibility?
faq.answer: It improves readability and contrast awareness, but it is not a full accessibility compliance audit.

faq.question: What kind of output should I expect?
faq.answer: Expect concise visual findings, preserved design intent, and small changes focused on production polish.
